arts.ttl: Is there VAR and extra time in FA Cup fifth round? Rules explained for Man United and Man City
arts.sum: Manchester United and Manchester City are in FA Cup fifth round action this weekend as they aim to reach the quarter-final of the competition
What makes an FA Cup giant-killer? BBC Sport looks at some of Plymouth's heroes in the competition so far.

arts.ttl: Man City's Pep Guardiola delivers blunt response to Javier Tebas accusations, La Liga legal complain
arts.sum: Pep Guardiola delivers a blunt response to accusations aimed at Man City from La Liga president Javier Tebas, who confirms that a legal complaint has been filed to the EU Commission against the Citizens.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich chief confirms they will try to sign Bayer Leverkusen star Florian Wirtz this summer - with Bundesliga giants set to battle Man City for 'the best player in Germany'
arts.sum: Bayern Munich chief Karl-Heinz Rummenigge has announced that his club will target the signing of Bayer Leverkusen's Florian Wirtz this summer.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola has his say on De Bruyne’s Man City future
arts.sum: Incredibly, Manchester City’s FA Cup game against Plymouth Argyle on Saturday represents their final chance of picking up silverware this season. There has been no movement on a contract extension for De Bruyne and, with injury issues becoming increasingly prominent, the Belgian great looks set to leave the club in the near future.

arts.ttl: Man City vs. Plymouth: "Giant" Pep Guardiola labelled "one of the most successful coaches in sports history" by Miron Muslic
arts.sum: Man City manager Pep Guardiola is described as "one of the most successful coaches in sports history" by Plymouth Argyle boss Miron Muslic ahead of Saturday's FA Cup fifth-round tie.

arts.ttl: Team News: Manchester City vs. Plymouth Argyle injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Saturday's FA Cup clash between Manchester City and Plymouth Argyle.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid fined for homophobic Guardiola chants
arts.sum: Real Madrid are fined by Uefa after some of their fans directed homophobic chants towards Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola in their Champions League second-leg tie at the Bernabeu.

arts.ttl: How bright is Man City's future? Assessing Guardiola's squad
arts.sum: This has been a disastrous season for Man City and Pep Guardiola, but the vibe around the club is that the future remains bright. Ahead of their FA Cup fifth-round clash, just how bright is it?

arts.ttl: Football Daily | Plymouth’s daunting task and men who think they could conquer a bear
arts.sum: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! In a 2021 study, 1,700 people were asked which animal they could beat in an unarmed fight. The results were alarming, with 9% of men believing they could conquer both an elephant (who regularly uproot trees to eat their leaves) and a gorilla (who have, on average, about six times more upper-body strength than the average human). Football Daily is not in the business (or any business, come to think of it) on how many men out of 100 would actually survive an unarmed scrap against an elephant or a gorilla, but this tea-timely email has been pondering if it really would be nine. But whether you’re signing up for a fight-to-the-death tussle against a wild animal or casually attempting to boot a stray football (first time) back over the playground wall in front of your partner, their parents and a small crowd of expectant teenagers, what people think they can do versus what they can actually do doesn’t always match up. Confidence is essential in elite sport, we’re told, but – just like those four pint glasses Football Daily dropped on the pub floor after refusing the offer of a tray – it also remains an incredibly fragile and dangerous commodity. Premier League content is so valuable because it’s so widely demanded. How many global platforms are there? Probably just Netflix. If you’re thinking about how do I launch a global product, you do it in partnership with content like this” – Todd Boehly overlooks the huge global reach of yours truly while also failing to acknowledge that it’s no longer 1992 – and the Premier League is doing all right, Jack. With regard to the nomenclature bandwagon (Football Daily letters passim), can I suggest that Old Trafford henceforth be known as the Theatre of Memes?” – Callum Taylor. Since Football Daily/Weekly are obsessed with the phrase ‘Farmers’ League’, I suggest having a look at the table of the Swiss Super League, where the top eight teams are within six points and the top four are tied with only goal difference separating them. That’s what a true Farmers’ League looks like. Also, the standout player of the season so far has been the former Liverpool and Stoke winger Xherdan Shaqiri with eight goals and 11 assists. I thought you might find this amusing” – Florian Schönmann. Can I ask why Jess Park has a smart phone in her hand (photo in yesterday’s News, Bits and Bobs, full email edition? Does she not believe in VAR confirming her goal?” – Michael Dawson. Continue reading...
